name: Vibes
summary: A high-performance terminal user interface (TUI) for planning and running autonomous AI agent workflows, built with Ink and React
goals: Deliver a responsive, keyboard-driven TUI for managing AI agent missions end-to-end
Support any OpenAI-compatible LLM backend (Ollama, LM Studio) with streaming, tool calls, and reasoning
Provide a DAG-based autonomous scheduler with parallel task execution and dependency resolution
Integrate deeply with MCP (Model Context Protocol) for external tool discovery and invocation
Maintain persistent long-term memory with structured taxonomy (semantic, episodic, procedural)
Augment agent context with Neo4j vector search (Codex RAG) for code pattern retrieval
primary_features: Hierarchical mission planning via LLM (goals → milestones → tasks)
Autonomous agent loop with streaming responses, tool calls, and reasoning-first thinking
DAG-based task scheduler with concurrent execution (up to 4 tasks) and dependency resolution
Interactive TUI with Dashboard, Mission Tree, Live Task View, Settings, and Log Stream
Built-in file system tools (read, write, edit, glob, grep, shell execution)
MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration with environment variable expansion in config
Structural codebase audit for pre-flight analysis (imports, dead code, prop checks)
Long-term memory with local JSONL backend or optional mem0ai remote storage
secondary_features: Triage agent for automatic error recovery and task re-routing
Multi-agent orchestration (Planner, Coder, Reviewer, Supervisor roles)
Codex RAG via Neo4j vector search for code pattern injection
Event hooks system (before_thinking, after_tool_call, on_error) with shell commands
Context compaction and token budget management for long-running sessions
Update notification with inline changelog viewer
Workspace undo (Alt+Z) via git hard-reset after failed missions
JSON repair utilities for LLM output sanitization

tech_stack.llm_sdk: OpenAI SDK v4 (compatible with Ollama, LM Studio)
tech_stack.rag: Neo4j vector search via Python subprocess (Codex RAG)

architecture.overview: React-based TUI using Ink for terminal rendering. The agent layer (mission-planner, scheduler, task-executor) orchestrates LLM-driven task execution with tool calls. MCP bridges external tool servers. Memory and Codex RAG enrich agent context. All state flows through React hooks to the Ink UI components.

agent.description: Core agent engine: mission planning, DAG scheduling, task execution, review, and triage

tui.description: Terminal UI built with Ink 6 + React 19, providing keyboard-driven views for all mission phases
tui.implementation: React functional components with Ink primitives

mcp.description: Model Context Protocol integration for external tool discovery and invocation via stdio/SSE
mcp.implementation: TypeScript (fastmcp) managing MCP subprocesses with env-var expansion

tools.description: Built-in agent tool registry: file operations, shell execution, code search, surgical editing

memory.description: Long-term memory with local JSONL persistence or optional mem0ai remote backend, structured as semantic/episodic/procedural
memory.implementation: TypeScript with memory taxonomy and token-budget-aware retrieval

multiAgent.description: Parallel multi-agent orchestration with distinct roles: Planner, Coder, Reviewer, Supervisor

phases.name: Core Agent & Scheduler
phases.description: Hierarchical planning, DAG scheduling, task execution loop, file tools, MCP integration

tasks.name: Task executor with streaming + tool calls

phases.name: TUI Polish & User Experience
phases.description: Refined keyboard navigation, settings editor, log views, trace inspection, intervention flow

tasks.name: Live task view with tool call groups

phases.name: Reliability, Memory & Multi-Agent
phases.description: Long-term memory, multi-agent orchestration, Codex RAG, triage agent hardening

todo.title: Harden session persistence with resume across restarts
Add keyboard shortcut hints in all view headers
Panel focus cycling polish (Tab between views)
Write comprehensive test suite for agent loop
Update README with current keyboard shortcut table

current_capabilities: Full mission lifecycle: plan → approve → execute → review → complete
Autonomous agent ReAct loop with streaming, tool calling, and structured outputs
DAG-based scheduling with up to 4 concurrent tasks and dependency resolution
Multi-pane terminal UI with keyboard navigation (Alt+keys)
Git auto-snapshot before missions, undo via hard-reset
MCP tool integration (GitHub, Perplexity, Neo4j, sequential-thinking)
Structural codebase audit (imports, dead code, prop mismatches)
Codex RAG via Neo4j vector search for code pattern augmentation
Context window compaction to stay within model limits
JSON output repair and sanitization for LLM responses
next_steps: Complete session persistence with full resume support
Polish panel focus cycling and keyboard shortcut hints
Implement adversarial audit phase for critical missions
Write and expand test coverage (unit + integration)
Set up CI/CD for automated validation and npm publishing

key_files.src/index.tsx: TUI entry point and app shell
key_files.src/vibes-cli.ts: CLI entry point for the vibes command
key_files.src/agent/scheduler.ts: DAG mission scheduler orchestrating all task execution
key_files.src/agent/task-executor.ts: Core agent loop (system prompt → tool calls → response)
key_files.src/agent/mission-planner.ts: LLM-based plan decomposition into milestones and tasks
key_files.src/tui/components/task-view.tsx: Live task execution view with tool call groups
key_files.src/mcp/mcp-service.ts: MCP subprocess manager and tool discovery
key_files.src/tools/file-tools.ts: File system tools for the agent

security_considerations: API keys stored in .env (never committed) with .env.example as template
MCP config supports ${VARIABLE} expansion from .env — no hardcoded secrets
Git auto-snapshot before missions enables safe undo
project.json itself should NOT be deployed to production — contains sensitive architectural info
open_questions: Should we adopt a dedicated state machine library for the agent loop?
What's the right compaction strategy for 128K+ token models?
Should multi-agent mode become the default for complex missions?
How to best handle model fallback when primary LLM is unreachable?
current_goals: Harden session persistence with resume support
Polish TUI keyboard navigation and focus handling
Expand test coverage for agent and scheduler modules

completed: Dashboard, Mission View, Task View, Settings
demo_ready: Full mission lifecycle with planner + scheduler + executor
future_enhancements: Built-in diff view for file edits
Fine-grained model routing per task type
